task_completion.description: Search the security_logs index in Splunk
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Search & Reporting interface with a query starting with or including 'index="security_logs"' or 'index=security_logs'.

task_completion.description: Formulate the SPL query with distinct user and ratio thresholds
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Splunk search query containing statistical functions for distinct users (e.g., 'dc(user)') and failed attempts, along with filtering logic checking for >= 5 users and a ratio of < 3.
Look for the 'Save As Alert' configuration modal or the Splunk Alerts page showing an alert explicitly named 'Password_Spray_Detection'.
Look for the Splunk Dashboards page or a dashboard view with the title 'Password_Spray_Dashboard'.

task_completion.description: Add a first visualization panel to the dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least one visualization panel (e.g., table, bar chart, timechart, or single value) rendered within the 'Password_Spray_Dashboard' depicting password spray activity.

task_completion.description: Add a second visualization panel to the dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a second, distinct visualization panel rendered within the 'Password_Spray_Dashboard', confirming that at least two panels are present on the dashboard.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Splunk Web GUI rather than bypassing it with terminal scripts
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Splunk Web interface in a browser (http://localhost:8000), rather than just terminal windows executing curl commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The dashboard was created within Splunk, not mocked up externally
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the standard Splunk dashboard UI elements (e.g., 'Edit', 'Export', Splunk navigation bar) surrounding the dashboard panels.

integrity.description: The visualizations render actual Splunk data or valid configurations
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for panels that show actual data results, axes, or valid Splunk search configurations, rather than empty placeholders or static text files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The alert must be named exactly 'Password_Spray_Detection'. The detection logic must check for a minimum of 5 distinct users (min_users: 5) and a maximum attempt ratio of 3 (max_ratio: 3). The dashboard must be named exactly 'Password_Spray_Dashboard' and contain at least 2 visualization panels (min_panels: 2).
